freeze_note: Publishing is frozen (fail-closed). The queue was ~95% synthetic 'illustrative' data (519/546 datasets) and premises had collapsed into searchable noun phrases. Nothing publishes until real, cited stories exist AND a human opts in (PUBLISH_ENABLED=1 / --publish / canary mode). See scripts/editorial_gate.py and docs/EDITORIAL_RESET.md.

guardrails.premise_bar: one real expectation-reversal + one consequential, verifiable number; a searchable noun phrase is an auto-reject

scoring_note: reaction_rate (comments + shares + subs per view) is now weighted heavily. The 2026-07 analytics showed videos that HELD attention (e.g. Hottest Planets: 412 views, 117% avg-viewed) still produced ~0 comments, shares, or subscribers. Retention without reaction = the content held the eye but generated no genuine interest. Optimize for 'would someone send this to a friend', not just watch-time.
current_directives: PUBLISHING IS FROZEN. Do not chase volume. Zero videos on a weak day beats four generic ones.
Every published claim needs a real source: publisher, retrieval date, unit, and a direct source-value -> narration mapping.
Reject weak premises at the title stage. 'Tectonic Plates on the Move' dies before render.
Run ONE experiment at a time: two canaries, one changed variable, a predetermined success metric, then keep or revert.
Argue AGAINST publishing. The default answer is 'not good enough'; a video must prove it deserves to ship.
rules_of_thumb: Action-framed topics beat passive-fact topics within the same category.
Body-facts and cost-shock topics outperform space on views/hour so far.
High average-view-duration with zero reactions means the premise was watchable but not interesting enough to act on. Fix the premise, not the polish.
A title that is a plain noun phrase (no verb, no number, no tension) is a reject regardless of how the video looks.
